skill.name: pitfalls-websocket
skill.description: Guides implementation and review of WebSocket servers and clients, with patterns for shared HTTP ports, heartbeat checks, reconnection, and message validation.
skill.summary: Review and improve WebSocket implementations with heartbeat, reconnection, and message handling patterns.
skill.author: BarisSozen

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown code fences, WebSocket example code, and error responses, but the reviewed file is documentation with non-executed TypeScript examples. No malicious intent, prompt injection, credential handling, command execution, or data exfiltration was found.

low_findings.title: Static command execution alerts dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are markdown code fences around TypeScript examples, not executable shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are markdown fence delimiters or starts of fenced TypeScript examples. No shell command execution syntax or runnable script file is present.

low_findings.title: WebSocket constructor is illustrative example code
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The WebSocket connection appears inside a client reconnection example. It is expected for this skill topic and is not code executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding section explicitly documents client reconnection logic. There is no hidden endpoint, credential transfer, or executable runtime path.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography alert dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged metadata line describes WebSocket topics and includes the trigger term wss. It does not implement or recommend a weak cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is skill metadata text, not cryptographic code. No weak hash, cipher, or TLS downgrade instruction appears in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance alerts dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged lines send simple invalid-message errors to a WebSocket client. They do not inspect the host system or expose system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are generic error responses in example message handlers. No commands, environment access, filesystem access, or host enumeration is present.

content.value_statement: WebSocket applications often fail from dropped connections, missing heartbeats, or weak message validation. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused review patterns for reliable real-time features.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether a WebSocket server shares the existing HTTP server port.
Explains heartbeat and ping-pong handling for stale connection cleanup.
Guides client reconnection design with exponential backoff.
Reviews message parsing and validation before dispatch.
Provides a compact checklist for WebSocket reliability reviews.
limitations: Does not provide a complete production WebSocket framework.
Does not cover authentication, authorization, or rate limiting in depth.
Does not test live network behavior or measure latency.
Examples are TypeScript-oriented and may need adaptation for other stacks.

use_cases.title: Review a real-time feature before launch
use_cases.description: Check a chat, notification, or collaboration feature for heartbeat, reconnection, and validation gaps.

use_cases.title: Debug recurring WebSocket disconnects
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to inspect stale connections, missing pings, and client reconnect behavior.

use_cases.title: Standardize WebSocket code reviews
use_cases.description: Apply a shared review pattern for server setup, client recovery, and message handling.

prompt_templates.title: Basic WebSocket Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of an existing implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my WebSocket implementation for heartbeat, reconnection, and message validation issues. Focus on simple reliability risks first.

prompt_templates.title: Client Reconnection Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review of browser or app client behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect this WebSocket client design for reconnection problems. Check backoff, retry reset behavior, and error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Server Reliability Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Production readiness review for server-side WebSocket handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this WebSocket server plan for shared HTTP server setup, stale connection cleanup, heartbeat intervals, and safe message parsing.

prompt_templates.title: End-to-End Real-Time Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review across server and client behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this real-time feature end to end. Identify failure modes across connection lifecycle, heartbeat behavior, reconnection strategy, and message validation.

output_examples.input: Review my chat WebSocket design for reliability risks.
output: Confirms whether the WebSocket server attaches to the existing HTTP server.
Flags missing heartbeat cleanup for stale connections.
Checks that the client uses bounded exponential backoff.

output_examples.input: Find message handling problems in my real-time notification service.
output: Identifies unvalidated message types before business logic runs.
Recommends clear handling for invalid payloads.
Separates connection errors from expected invalid client messages.

best_practices: Validate every incoming message before dispatching it to application logic.
Use heartbeat checks to terminate stale connections on a predictable interval.
Reset reconnection attempts after a successful client connection.
anti_patterns: Creating a separate WebSocket port without a clear deployment reason.
Retrying client connections immediately without backoff or a maximum delay.
Trusting parsed message contents without checking the expected type.

faq.question: Does this skill execute WebSocket code?
faq.answer: No. It provides review guidance and TypeScript-oriented examples for WebSocket implementations.

faq.question: Can it help with dropped WebSocket connections?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on heartbeat checks, stale connection cleanup, and client reconnection behavior.

faq.question: Does it include authentication guidance?
faq.answer: Only indirectly. It focuses on reliability patterns, not full authentication or authorization design.

faq.question: Which languages does it fit best?
faq.answer: The examples use TypeScript concepts, but the review checklist applies to most WebSocket stacks.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use it for code review?
faq.answer: Yes. It gives concrete review points for server setup, heartbeat logic, reconnection, and validation.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace publishing?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file is documentation with examples, and no executable malicious behavior was found.
